cityDNA.coreFantasy: A large feline beastfolk city where Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential, and Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws turn forest, root network, old growth woodland, dry ground and limestone blocks, jade tile, obsidian, canvas, turquoise inlay into a distinct civic machine.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Vhal’Tigris should feel like a greatcat civic terrace where sacred nature, temples, public works, craft, and high residences balance grace with watchfulness.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Vhal’Tigris organizes public life around alert stewardship: its districts convert Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential into a readable system of authority, labor, memory, and movement.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Vhal’Tigris should be recognized by Limestone Blocks, Jade Tile, Obsidian, Canvas, Turquoise Inlay, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Hidden Thresholds, Law Binding, Threshold Oaths, and the named anchors Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws, Sacred Grove Beacon, and Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: The city belongs to large feline beastfolk; its civic habits should grow from the sidecar-backed district spine rather than generic fantasy urbanism.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws, Sacred Grove Beacon, Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, and the five section landmark families symbolize alert stewardship while warning against confidence becoming aristocratic entitlement.

canonLocks: Vhal’Tigris is a large feline beastfolk city, not a generic settlement with cosmetic motifs.
The Wonder is Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws.
The Beacon is Sacred Grove Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, located at the District 13 authority center.
The five district sections are Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential.
The board should be treated as twenty-five district slots grouped into five section identities.
Section 1 (Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove) preserves these exact anchor names: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook.
Section 2 (Religious / Temple Quarter) preserves these exact anchor names: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook.
Section 3 (Public Works Authority) preserves these exact anchor names: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook.
Section 4 (Artisan Guilds) preserves these exact anchor names: Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook.
Section 5 (High Residential) preserves these exact anchor names: High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook.
provisionalCanon: Individual district-slot names beyond exact sidecar anchors are organizational labels for the 25-seat board, not new landmark canon.
Minor street customs should be derived from the section spine unless future source files provide more exact anchors.
rejectedReads: Vhal’Tigris as a generic fantasy city without its named Wonder, Beacon, and Seat of Power.
Replacing exact sidecar anchor names with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, wall, or archive.
Treating district section labels as decorative instead of civic systems that drive scenes and assets.
cautionFlags: Preserve exact named anchors from sidecars.
Use the culture identity respectfully as civic design language rather than costume.
Keep all 25 district slots legible even when five slots share one section identity.
Do not add unsupported individual district landmark names beyond the source-backed anchors.

environment.environmentalLogic: Vhal’Tigris's environment should make Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential feel physically necessary. Terrain, light, civic routes, and landmark placement must support movement through all five section identities rather than read as interchangeable scenery.

skyline: Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws is the wonder-scale skyline identifier.
Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court marks the central authority mass at District 13.
Sacred Grove Beacon must remain visible as the public signal landmark.
Section landmarks should form a readable ring of Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential.
infrastructure: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall

civilization.people: Vhal’Tigris's people are large feline beastfolk; they should be written through shared civic habits, public spaces, work rhythms, and the visible demands of the five section identities.
socialClasses: officials and stewards tied to Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
landmark keepers and civic hall administrators
street vendors, porters, runners, and maintenance crews
families living around the section-specific local anchors
civilization.governance: Authority centers on Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court and is made visible through section civic halls, named landmarks, permissions, public signals, and the citywide response to Sacred Grove Beacon.
civilization.religion: Religious or ceremonial life should be shown through source-backed shrines, temples, wonders, oath spaces, ancestor sites, or ritual geometry when those appear in the district spine.
customs: citizens orient themselves by the section identity of a street before they identify an address
public notices and signals are interpreted through the Beacon and civic halls
named landmarks act as meeting points, proof points, and local memory anchors
section-specific work routines shape food, clothing, posture, and street traffic
District 13 authority matters even when scenes occur in outer district slots
bodyLanguage: citizens check landmarks and signals before crossing into another section
officials use the posture and tools of their section role
workers treat section anchors as practical wayfinding points
crowds become more formal near the Seat of Power and more local near minor landmarks
speechPatterns: directions use exact landmark names rather than generic descriptions
official speech references permits, seals, routes, oaths, or signals as appropriate to the city
working speech is concrete, location-heavy, and tied to civic function
locals distinguish between the Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section landmarks without confusion

streets.appearance: Street scenes should make Vhal’Tigris immediately identifiable through Limestone Blocks, Jade Tile, Obsidian, Canvas, Turquoise Inlay, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground, Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Hidden Thresholds, Law Binding, Threshold Oaths, and the exact section anchors rather than generic fantasy facades.
streets.movement: Movement flows through five section identities and twenty-five district slots. People navigate by Sacred Grove Beacon, Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, section civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks.
streets.lighting: Lighting should use Mist Veils, White Limestone Civic Blocks, Sun Disc Reliefs, Lantern Lit, Civic Blue Glow, symbolic civic object, with the Beacon and Seat carrying distinct civic emphasis.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should interact with forest, root network, old growth woodland, dry ground and the city's source-backed materials, altering visibility, travel speed, public gathering, and signal readability.
streets.dayCycle: Daytime emphasizes section labor: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential. Civic halls, shops, and public works create the main public rhythm.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes should preserve the city's identity through landmark silhouettes, beacon light, watch routes, late shops, ritual spaces, and section-specific sounds.

common: nature preserve / sacred grove supply stalls
uncommon: nature preserve / sacred grove specialist counters
prestigious: workshops or offices near Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
specialist houses serving Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws
master artisans tied to section landmarks

housing.wealthy: High-status housing clusters near Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court, major terraces, and prestigious landmark approaches; it should display the city materials cleanly and control sightlines toward Wonder or Beacon.
housing.middle: Middle housing occupies upper rooms, side courts, work-family compounds, and mixed-use streets close to civic halls and section services.
housing.poor: Poorer housing should be practical and specific to local labor, with patched materials, shared courtyards, crowded lanes, and strong dependence on nearby section anchors.

wonder.name: Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of alert stewardship made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A wonder-scale skyline anchor using Limestone Blocks, Jade Tile, Obsidian, Canvas, Turquoise Inlay, Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground, and the large feline beastfolk visual language.
wonder.civicRole: Represents the whole city above the five sections: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential.
storyHooks: access to Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws changes public legitimacy
a citywide ritual reveals hidden pressure
the Wonder becomes a navigation or warning reference

beacon.name: Sacred Grove Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public signal and shared alarm for Vhal’Tigris.
beacon.visualRole: A section-scale signal landmark that must read immediately as the city Beacon.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates public response, civic warnings, and Conjunction-state attention.
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a signal
the Beacon redirects traffic between sections
a warning exposes which citizens trust civic authority

districtDioramaGuidance: Create twenty-five district dioramas grouped into five recognizable sections: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential.
Each district should use its districtNumber and sectionNumber, but avoid baked text labels in imagery.
D13 must read as the Seat of Power district centered on Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
Repeated section anchors may appear across five related slots, but each slot needs its own street function, crowd density, and prop emphasis.
Use exact source-backed anchor names for file naming, prompt references, and canon cross-checking.
S1 must preserve Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove with anchors: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook.
S2 must preserve Religious / Temple Quarter with anchors: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook.
S3 must preserve Public Works Authority with anchors: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook.
S4 must preserve Artisan Guilds with anchors: Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall, Woven Banners Monument, Masterwork Arcade Nook.
S5 must preserve High Residential with anchors: High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, Salon Pavilion Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Map backgrounds should keep Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws as the skyline symbol, Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court at the central civic authority, and Sacred Grove Beacon as the readable public signal.

Arrange the five section identities so Vhal’Tigris does not converge with other cities: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Public Works Authority, Artisan Guilds, High Residential.

Use terrain cues (Forest, Root Network, Old Growth Woodland, Dry Ground), material cues (Limestone Blocks, Jade Tile, Obsidian, Canvas, Turquoise Inlay), and magic cues (Beast Bonding, Ancestor Guidance, Hidden Thresholds, Law Binding, Threshold Oaths) consistently across district clusters.
Show roads, terraces, gates, courts, or paths that make the twenty-five-district tactical layout plausible.
requiredVisualElements: Greatcat Sunstripe Hunt Terrace of the Golden Paws
Watchful Eye Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
elementsToAvoid: extra top-level schema sections or embedded generation notes
generic fantasy city assets that ignore exact anchor names
unlabeled invented landmarks replacing sidecar names
flattening five sections into one visual district
treating culture identity as costume rather than civic design
overcrowded detail that hides Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power
